content: built a free task breaker for people with ADHD. you paste in a task. it breaks it into 5-10 tiny steps. no login. no ads. no bs. just smaller tasks you can actually start.
goblin tools has a waitlist now. built a free alternative that does the same thing. breaks tasks into steps. works instantly. tasksmash.surge.sh
what task is sitting on your list that you keep skipping? (reply with it and i'll show you how to break it down. also built a free tool that does this automatically)
tracked my output for 60 days. the days i hit 3 tasks beat the days i had 15 on the list. every time. built a tool around this. coreday.surge.sh
most productivity apps make you track habits. this one doesn't. pick 3 non-negotiables for today. hit them. done. nothing else to fill out. coreday.surge.sh
how many tasks do you actually complete on a heavy list day vs a 3-task day? i bet the number isn't what you think. (built a free tool for the 3-task approach)
built a pomodoro timer that actually keeps score. 25 min sessions tracked over time. you can see exactly how many deep work hours you've logged this week. focuslock-web.surge.sh
free pomodoro timer with a stats dashboard. 25 min on. 5 min off. tracks your sessions so you can see your actual output, not just time spent online. focuslock-web.surge.sh
what kills your focus more. too many tasks open at once or notifications? curious what the split is. (also here's a free timer i built that tracks your sessions)

took a quiz that told me i was using the wrong productivity system for how my brain works. built a version of that quiz. 5 questions. recommends a specific free tool at the end. productivity-stack-quiz.surge.sh
free quiz that tells you which productivity tool actually fits how you work. not generic advice. specific recommendation based on your answers. takes 2 minutes. productivity-stack-quiz.surge.sh
are you bad at productivity or just using the wrong system? (legit question. 5-question quiz figures it out and points you to a free tool that matches your brain)
